brand_identity.name: Elevideo
brand_identity.description: A SaaS platform that transforms horizontal videos into vertical social media content instantly.

headings.usage: Use for all page titles, section headers, and modal titles. Tracking tight (-0.02em).

body.usage: Use for all paragraphs, labels, inputs, and data tables. Tracking normal.

usage_rules.primary_action: Use Accent color for 'Process Video', 'Save', 'Upload' buttons.
usage_rules.destructive: Use Red-500 for 'Delete Project' or 'Cancel Job'.

grid_system.video_detail: grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-8 (2 cols for video player, 1 col for controls)

navigation.mobile: Bottom Sheet or Hamburger Menu (Sheet component)

VideoCard.style: Card component with image aspect-video on top, title and status badge below. Minimalist borders.

ProjectCard.style: Simple clean card with project title, video count, and last updated date. Hover effect: border-accent/50.

ProcessingPanel.style: Split view. Left: Video Player. Right: Processing Options (Form).
ProcessingPanel.feedback: Show progress bar or spinner when processing.

icons.style: Stroke width 1.5px or 2px. Size 16-20px for UI, 24px for navigation.

instructions_to_main_agent: Implement the 'Outfit' font for all headings and 'Inter' for body.
Create a ThemeProvider to handle Light/Dark mode toggling using 'next-themes'.
Use 'TanStack Query' for all API calls (Projects, Videos, Jobs).
For the Video Processing Page (/projects/:id/videos/:id), use a split layout on desktop: Video Player (Left) + Processing Form (Right). On mobile, stack them.
Ensure all forms (Login, Register, Upload) use 'react-hook-form' and 'zod' for validation.
Use 'sonner' for all success/error notifications (e.g., 'Video processing started').
Authentication: Store JWT in localStorage or cookies, and use an Axios interceptor to attach it to requests.
Mobile-First: Always start styling with mobile classes (base) and add 'md:' or 'lg:' for larger screens.
Do NOT use gradients for backgrounds. Keep it flat and clean.
Add 'data-testid' to all buttons, inputs, and forms for testing.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES: Don't make generic centered layouts. Use grids and asymmetry where appropriate.
Use 2-3x more spacing than typical (e.g., gap-6 instead of gap-2).
Micro-interactions: Add hover states to all interactive elements.
Avoid 'AI Slop': Do not use generic AI emojis or standard bootstrap grids.
Ensure contrast ratios meet WCAG AA standards.
